title: Korean herbal medicine scaffold cross-reference: a literature-grounded scaffold atlas linking 동의보감 and 향약집성방 monographs to modern target classes via 60 ns molecular dynamics evidence
abstract: Korean traditional medicine texts (동의보감 1610, 향약집성방 1433) catalog hundreds of herbal monographs with empirical clinical use across centuries, but systematic cross-reference between these monographs and contemporary molecular target classes is fragmentary. We assemble a scaffold-level atlas mapping 동의보감 / 향약집성방 monograph entries to (i) the principal natural-product scaffold(s) reported in modern phytochemistry, (ii) target classes implicated in skin-fibrosis, pigmentation, alopecia, and acne pathways from public databases (ChEMBL, Open Targets), and (iii) molecular-dynamics evidence at the 60 ns timescale for the top scaffold-target pair in each disease class. Cross-references are framed as hypothesis-generating only — no clinical efficacy claim is made. The atlas is published as supplementary material to the Genesis_Medicine preprint series (DOIs 10.5281/zenodo.20018254 through 20018378). Keywords: Korean traditional medicine, 동의보감, 향약집성방, scaffold atlas, natural products, molecular dynamics, MMP-1, target class.

authors.name: HanCheongWoo
affiliations: Genesis_Medicine Lab, Seoul, Republic of Korea

prior_history: v0.2 still being finalized 2026-05-08; previously held back from Zenodo deposit pending 60 ns MD evidence completion. Releasing on OSF as v0.2 in current state.
disclaimer: In silico only. No clinical claim. Wet-lab and IRB pending.
competing_interests: Author is founder of HAN PREDICT, Inc. and operator of Recover Korean Medicine Clinic. No commercial product is sold based on these results.

ai_disclosure.note: Author verified all claims; AI not used for data or hypotheses.
